name: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy
sources: Candidate for verification: SAGES Safe Cholecystectomy Multi-Society Practice Guideline (2020)
Strasberg SM, Critical View of Safety
review_note: Not yet clinician-verified. First candidate for promotion to verified.
labels: Lower gallbladder dissected from liver bed

intake.why: Determines composition and how much can be shown clearly
One figure cannot show everything; focus drives which structures dominate

figure_scopes.overview: Biliary tree and Calot triangle in one plate, with an optional inset zoom of the triangle
figure_scopes.storyboard: Multi-panel: exposure -> hepatocystic triangle dissection -> CVS -> clip/divide -> gallbladder separation
figure_scopes.closeup: Single Critical View of Safety panel

laterality: Right upper quadrant; gallbladder on the inferior surface of liver segments IVb/V
must_have: Gallbladder fundus, body, infundibulum/neck, and inferior liver edge.
Cystic duct, common hepatic duct, common bile duct, common hepatic artery, proper hepatic artery, right hepatic artery, and cystic artery relationship.
Usual cystic artery course: branch from the right hepatic artery, pass through the hepatocystic triangle, and run toward the gallbladder neck/body.
Hepatocystic triangle / Calot triangle shown as a real anatomic space, not a decorative outline.
Critical View of Safety panel with lower gallbladder separated from the liver bed.
spatial_rules: Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct.
Common hepatic artery continues as the proper hepatic artery, which divides into right and left hepatic arteries; the cystic artery usually arises from the right hepatic artery.
Cystic artery lies in or near the hepatocystic triangle and approaches the gallbladder neck before supplying the gallbladder wall.
Only cystic duct and cystic artery should enter the gallbladder before division.
Common bile duct remains continuous and untouched in clip/divide panels.
Right hepatic artery should continue toward the right liver, not be clipped or mistaken for the cystic artery.

forbidden_errors.error: Common bile duct clipped or divided.
Cystic duct confused with common bile duct.
Right hepatic artery clipped, divided, or mislabeled as the cystic artery.
A third duct shown entering the gallbladder in the CVS panel.
Cystic artery placed far outside the hepatocystic triangle without explanation.
Cystic artery drawn traveling into the left hepatic lobe instead of toward the gallbladder neck/body.

safety_concept: Achieve the Critical View of Safety before dividing any duct or artery; if CVS cannot be achieved, depict a bail-out concept rather than unsafe division.
phases: Gallbladder exposure and fundus/infundibulum retraction to open the hepatocystic triangle.
Dissection and clearing of the hepatocystic / Calot triangle.
Critical View of Safety verification: only cystic duct and cystic artery enter the gallbladder; lower gallbladder separated from the liver bed.
Clip and divide the cystic duct and cystic artery; common bile duct untouched.
Gallbladder separation from the liver bed and specimen extraction.
verify: Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct; CBD continuous and uninstrumented.
Cystic artery arises from the right hepatic artery in the usual depiction, traverses the hepatocystic triangle, and runs toward the gallbladder neck/body.
Right hepatic artery continues toward the right liver and is not clipped or divided.
CVS panel shows only two structures entering the gallbladder and the lower third off the liver bed.
No extra/third duct entering the gallbladder.
